name: DELETED: Avoid data fields binded to columns to return sensitive data via APIs
description: The rule will raise a violation if any fields that user defines as sensitive is binded to a table column and is not annotated with @JsonIgnore for returning
rationale:
Exploitation of Excessive Data Exposure is simple, and is usually performed by sniffing the traffic to analyze the API responses, looking for sensitive data exposure that should not be returned to the user.
Since APIs are used as data sources, sometimes developers try to implement them in a generic way without thinking about the sensitivity of the exposed data. 
When binding data fields accessed by APIs to columns it must be cautiously controlled that what data is to be ignored when returning the field or the model
remediation: Implement a schema-based response validation mechanism as an extra layer of security. As part of this mechanism define and enforce data returned by all API methods, including errors.
total: All fields binded with Data Columns and classified as sensitive by User
alternativeName: DELETED: Avoid data fields binded to columns to return sensitive data via APIs
